natural_language: Seven blocks, A through G, in three separate piles on the table. Pile number one: C is on B, which is on A, which touches the table. Pile number two: E sits on D, D on the table. Pile number three: G rests on F, F on the table. The exposed surfaces — C, E, G — are all clear and grabbable. The hand starts empty. Now for the rearrangement. We want to end up with only two piles, not three. The new left pile: G on top of E, which is on top of C, which is on A, which stays on the table. The new right pile: F on D, which is on B, B on the table. So essentially we're merging the three piles into two by redistributing the blocks.
